behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: history manager/request builder -> LLM client payload (not just pure slicing logic). A unit test on a trim helper can miss wiring bugs where untrimmed history is still sent. Integration is enough with an in-process LLM stub capturing the outbound request. Rejected System: behavior text is about payload contents, not real network/OS/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no dependence on model judgment quality; this is deterministic trimming.
behaviors.test_description: Run chatbot service with real history-trimming and request-building modules wired together plus a stub LLM transport. Provide >10-message history, send a message, and assert the actual payload sent to the LLM contains only the latest 10 messages in correct order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output quality (relevance/on-topicness), which is non-deterministic and cannot be validated by fixed mocks without hiding real failures. Rejected Integration: stubs only test routing/wiring, not whether the model actually answers the question. Rejected Workflow: this behavior is a single generation-quality judgment, not a required multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM calls on a curated prompt set of customer questions, run multiple trials, and score responses for relevance/off-topic rate with a strict rubric (or independent evaluator model + human spot checks). Fail if relevance drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is tone judgment correctness ("correctly identifies ... too formal"), which depends on model-like language judgment; deterministic mocks can hide misclassification. Keep Agentic as minimum. Rejected Workflow: behavior can be validated with one real tone-judgment step plus verifying regen trigger, without requiring end-to-end validation of multiple model decisions in sequence. Rejected System: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess failure mode beyond the model call itself.
behaviors.test_description: Feed casual-tier cases with responses that are borderline/formal, execute real tone-check logic with real model dependency, and assert formal responses are flagged and regeneration is triggered. Track false negatives/positives across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module persistence correctness (chat flow + persistence layer + schema/transaction handling). A pure unit test is too low because real failure appears at module boundaries and DB mapping. Integration with a real local test DB catches missing fields/order/transaction mistakes. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: correctness is about stored records/timestamps, not model output quality. Rejected System: behavior does not require real external infra beyond local DB wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Execute chat flow (including a forced regeneration path) against a real test database instance (e.g., sqlite/postgres test container), then assert all exchange records exist, include both drafts/final as specified, and timestamps are present, ordered, and tied to correct message IDs.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is orchestration/state handoff between tone-check result, regeneration result, and outbound presenter. This is multi-module control flow and can be caught with deterministic fixtures. Rejected Agentic using behavior text "When the tone check triggers regeneration": the trigger decision itself is preconditioned; test target is replacement/visibility logic, not model judgment quality. Rejected Workflow: no need to validate multiple model decisions, only post-trigger state replacement semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire controller, tone-check gate, regeneration handler, and response presenter with deterministic stubs; force a regeneration-trigger outcome and assert only regenerated response is returned to client channel while the rejected draft is withheld (but may remain internal/audit-only per design).
